title: Leveraging Self-supervised representation learning in Detecting adverse drug events using electronic health records (EHR)
authors: H. Davis, T. Jones, E. Brown
abstract: In recent years, the clinical domain has seen an exponential rise in unstructured text data. This study investigates the efficacy of Self-supervised representation learning specifically tailored for detecting adverse drug events within electronic health records (EHR). Historically, manual extraction of such details has been resource-intensive and error-prone. We processed a comprehensive corpus from datasets spanning the 2023 to 2024 timeline. Our results demonstrate that using Self-supervised representation learning yields significant improvements over traditional baseline methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance in F1-score and robustness. Furthermore, error analysis reveals that handling domain-specific terminology remains a critical challenge. This work highlights the potential of deploying clinical AI systems in real-world healthcare settings to assist decision support and research trials.

Our results demonstrate that using Self-supervised representation learning yields significant improvements over traditional baseline methods, achieving state-of-the-art performance in F1-score and robustness. Furthermore, error analysis reveals that handling domain-specific terminology remains a critical challenge. This work highlights the potential of deploying clinical AI systems in real-world healthcare settings to assist decision support and research trials.
